original_hypothesis.title: Timelock Bypass by Compromised Admin Role
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The TaikoTimelockController allows any address with the TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE to bypass the minimum delay entirely. If the Security Council multisig (which holds this role) is compromised, attackers can execute malicious upgrades instantly.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The getMinDelay override explicitly returns 0 for the admin role. While intended for emergency response, it creates a single point of failure where an off-chain compromise translates to instant, unmitigable on-chain protocol takeover, defeating the purpose of the timelock.
deployment_assumptions: Security Council multisig is granted TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE
preconditions: Security Council multisig is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Timelock delay is conditionally disabled for specific roles

operational_questions: Is the Security Council multisig threshold sufficient to prevent compromise?
Are there off-chain monitoring systems that can detect and respond to instant execution?
validation_requirements: Verify the deployment script grants the role
Simulate an instant execution by the admin role

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromise the Security Council multisig off-chain
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Execute the payload instantly in the same block, bypassing the 7-day delay
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Drain protocol assets or alter core logic

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Total loss of funds and protocol integrity

execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: Social engineering or advanced key theft

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
